source_clause_title: 12.4 Community Right to Know

entities.name: Con Edison
entities.evidenceQuote: Con Edison

entities.name: Power Authority
entities.evidenceQuote: Power Authority

entities.name: Develop and maintain chemical inventory subject to reporting requirements
entities.evidenceQuote: Con Edison shall develop and maintain an inventory of all chemicals and hazardous substances, subject to reporting requirements under the Community Right To Know Law

entities.name: Notify Power Authority within five business days of new or removed chemicals
entities.evidenceQuote: Con Edison shall notify the Power Authority within five (5) business days whenever a new such chemical or hazardous substance is brought on to the facility or whenever such chemical or hazardous substance is removed from the facility

entities.name: Prepare, submit, and maintain the Risk Management Plan (RMP)
entities.evidenceQuote: The Power Authority shall be responsible for: Preparing, submitting, and maintaining the Risk Management Plan ("RMP") for the Facility in compliance with Environmental Laws

entities.name: Conduct periodic inspections to monitor compliance with the RMP
entities.evidenceQuote: The Power Authority shall be responsible for: Conducting periodic inspections of the Facility to monitor compliance with the RMP

entities.name: Provide Con Edison draft RMP at least 30 days prior to O&M Commencement Date
entities.evidenceQuote: At least thirty (30) days prior to the O&M Commencement Date, the Power Authority shall provide Con Edison with a draft RMP for Con Edison's review and comment

entities.name: Con Edison approval of RMP implementation provisions
entities.evidenceQuote: Provisions relating to Con Edison's implementation of the RMP, or any subsequent modification of implementation provisions, are subject to Con Edison's approval

entities.name: Implement the RMP after review and approval
entities.evidenceQuote: Con Edison shall be responsible for the implementation of the RMP so long as Con Edison has had an opportunity to review and approve the implementation sections of the RMP

entities.name: Obtain and submit Material Safety Data Sheets for EHS and OSHA hazardous substances within 3 months
entities.evidenceQuote: The Power Authority shall obtain and submit Material Safety Data Sheets ("MSDS") for any Extremely Hazardous Substance ("EHS") present at the facility ... to the State Emergency Response Commission ("SERC"), the Local Emergency Planning Committee ("LEPC"), and the local fire department within three (3) months of their acquisition

entities.name: Appoint Emergency Coordinator and notify SERC and LEPC within 60 days of EHS presence
entities.evidenceQuote: The Power Authority shall appoint an Emergency Coordinator and shall notify the SERC and the LEPC of such appointment within 60 days of an EHS being present at the facility

entities.name: File Facility Inventory Forms, 209-U forms, and other hazardous substance disclosure forms after O&M Commencement Date and pay fees
entities.evidenceQuote: After the O&M Commencement Date, the Power Authority shall be responsible for the filing of all Facility Inventory Forms ("FIF"), 209-U forms and any other forms relating to the disclosure of hazardous substances ... and for the payment of all associated filing fees

entities.name: Provide Con Edison with copies of filings
entities.evidenceQuote: and shall provide: Con Edison with a copy of each such filing

entities.name: Provide Power Authority with chemical inventory information in sufficient time
entities.evidenceQuote: Con Edison shall be responsible for providing Power Authority with information related to the chemical inventory under Con Edison's custody and control at the Facility in sufficient time to allow Power Authority to complete the required forms

entities.name: Chemical inventory development deadline

source_clause_title: 3.1.7 Management of Critical Infrastructure Protection (CIP) Standards:

entities.name: Power Authority
entities.evidenceQuote: Power Authority

entities.name: Con Edison
entities.evidenceQuote: Con Edison

entities.name: Determine whether the Astoria Annex Substation is a Critical Asset and determine the existence of Critical Cyber Assets prior to the O&M Commencement Date
entities.evidenceQuote: Prior to the O&M Commencement Date, NYPA will determine whether the Astoria Annex Substation is a Critical Asset and determine the existence of Critical Cyber Assets.

entities.name: Apply CIP-003 through CIP-009 compliance and perform required design and operational changes at Power Authority's sole cost and expense
entities.evidenceQuote: If Power Authority determines that the Astoria Annex Substation is a Critical Asset with Critical Cyber Assets, then compliance required by CIP-003 through CIP-009 shall be applied and any required changes to the existing design and operation of the Facility shall be the Power Authority's sole responsibility and performed at the Power Authority's sole cost and expense.

entities.name: Completion of design and operational changes at least sixty (60) days prior to the O&M Commencement Date
entities.evidenceQuote: all such design and operational changes required to comply with the obligations of being a Critical Cyber Asset must be completed at least sixty (60) days prior to the O&M Commencement Date.

entities.name: Complete all design and operational changes required to comply with obligations of being a Critical Cyber Asset
entities.evidenceQuote: all such design and operational changes required to comply with the obligations of being a Critical Cyber Asset must be completed at least sixty (60) days prior to the O&M Commencement Date.

entities.name: Con Edison shall adhere to its policies and procedures and provide Power Authority with documentary evidence to demonstrate compliance
entities.evidenceQuote: Con Edison shall be required to adhere to Con Edison's policies and procedures, and Con Edison shall perform all functional activities related to NERC CIP compliance and shall provide the Power Authority with documentary evidence required to demonstrate compliance

entities.name: Con Edison will manage protections, maintain compliance evidence, and manage compliance audits after Compliance Transfer Periods
entities.evidenceQuote: After the Compliance Transfer Periods, Con Edison will assume responsibility for managing those protections, maintaining the required compliance evidence, and managing compliance audits in accordance with Con Edison's processes.

entities.name: Con Edison shall establish and manage protections required by CIP-002 through CIP-009 at Power Authority's direction and sole cost and expense if protections are not necessary until after O&M Commencement Date
entities.evidenceQuote: If the protections required by CIP-002 through CIP-009 are determined by Power Authority, in its sole discretion, to not be necessary until after the O&M Commencement Date, then at Power Authority's direction, Con Edison shall be responsible at Power Authority's sole cost and expense, for establishing and managing the protections required by CIP-002 through CIP-009

entities.name: NYPA will retain responsibility for determining whether the Astoria Annex Substation is a Critical Asset and existence of Critical Cyber Assets
entities.evidenceQuote: NYPA, as the registered Transmission Owner for the Astoria Annex Substation, will retain responsibility for the determination of whether the Astoria Annex Substation is a Critical Asset and determining the existence of Critical Cyber Assets.

entities.name: Any changes required to upgrade the Facility shall be at the Power Authority's sole cost and expense
entities.evidenceQuote: Any changes required to upgrade the Facility shall be at the Power Authority's sole cost and expense.

relationships.evidenceQuote: Prior to the O&M Commencement Date, NYPA will determine whether the Astoria Annex Substation is a Critical Asset and determine the existence of Critical Cyber Assets.

entities.evidenceQuote: the Power Authority shall install and maintain, at its sole cost and expense, two dedicated communication lines between the Astoria Annex Substation and the Con Edison Energy Control Center ("Con Edison ECC") and such other communication lines as are necessary to operate any required fire detection and alarm systems

properties.frequency: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year

entities.evidenceQuote: Con Edison shall maintain continuous communication 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, and 365 days of the year, only through the Con Edison ECC

entities.evidenceQuote: Con Edison ECC shall notify the designated the Power Authority Energy Control Center ("NYPA ECC") dispatcher, of any emergency situation at the Astoria Annex Substation as soon as practicable

entities.name: Install and maintain a regular phone line for all outside calls
entities.evidenceQuote: the Power Authority shall install and maintain, at its sole cost and expense, a regular phone line for all outside calls
